Title: Garen Crownguard
Age: Current26 years old Timeline Born971 AN Edit

children.title: Early Life
children.textContent:
Garen was born in High Silvermere to Pieter and Augatha of the Crownguard family, one of the most influential noble houses in Demacia as long-standing defenders of the throne. Garen's father and his aunt Tianna were both decorated officers in the Demacian military, with Tianna serving as sword-captain of the Dauntless Vanguard, and it was expected that Garen would one day follow in their footsteps to defend the crown prince, Jarvan IV.

Like most Demacians, Garen and his younger sister Luxanna were raised on the stories of their kingdom's Founding, and the family paid regular pilgrimages to the Galio monument outside the capital. They also learned how the original settlers had fled the ashes and destruction of the Rune Wars. Their uncle, a ranger-knight of Demacia, frequently ventured beyond the kingdom's borders and brought home stories of the many dangers of the outside world. This uncle was later killed in action by a mage before he was eleven, and after seeing the fear in his sister's eyes, Garen resolved that magic was the greatest threat to Demacia, and that his life's mission would be to protect his kingdom and his family from it.

One day, however, while Garen and Lux were playing with their toy soldiers and mages, the two siblings got into an argument. Very suddenly, Lux accidentally shot a flash of light from her fingertips, and Garen, blinded momentarily, was left bewildered by what had happened. Their mother Augatha intervened and quickly took Lux away to speak with her, and ordered Garen to immediately forget what he had seen. Frightened, Garen convinced himself that it had been his imagination, and that Lux was not a mage.

children.title: Joining the Military
children.textContent:
At the age of twelve, Garen left High Silvermere to join the military as a squire. Garen's training was intense, and it was here that he befriended the young prince Jarvan IV. The two were of similar age and proved a formidable team, with Jarvan relying on Garen's determination and fortitude, and Garen relying on the prince's tactics. The two became inseparable among the other recruits, and Garen knew that he would one day serve Jarvan after he became king.

Garen participated in many campaigns in the years that followed. By the time he was eighteen, Garen had become one of his battalion's most fearsome warriors, having served in campaigns at the Freljordian borders, played a key role in purging cultists from the Silent Forest, and fought alongside the defenders of Whiterock.

children.title: Dauntless Vanguard Trials
children.textContent:
Shortly after his aunt Tianna's elevation to the rank of High Marshal, which occurred after the Battle of the Gates of Mourning, Garen and his battalion were summoned to meet King Jarvan III in the Hall of Valor in the Great City, where they were honored before the royal court. Here, Tianna recommended that the king allow Garen to undergo the necessary trials to join the ranks of the Dauntless Vanguard, an elite force of only the greatest soldiers in all of Demacia.

Garen went to the Crownguard manor to prepare for the Testing. Here, he met again with his parents and Lux, who had grown up in his absence. Garen had always suspected that she possessed magical powers, but now that he was older and their family was all together again in the capital, this fact had become much harder to ignore. As Lux and Augatha worked in service of the king and the Illuminators, Garen threw himself into his training and his duties to avoid confronting the truth, and he passed his trials with excellent scores. His entire family and Prince Jarvan were present for his inauguration into the Vanguard, where he took his oaths before the throne.

children.title: The Noxian Ambush
children.textContent:
While training in the eastern borderlands, Garen and four other Dauntless Vanguard were ambushed by five rogue Noxian mages. The mercenaries trapped Garen underwater and attempted to drown him, and he could only watch as his four friends were killed on the surface. Fueled by rage, Garen broke free from his magical bonds and pursued the mages, slaying four of them until only one remained. Though Garen had the last Noxian at the end of his blade, he decided to spare him as a display of Demacian justice: the four Demacians had been avenged with the lives of four Noxians, and like Garen, the final mercenary would be the lone survivor. The Noxian fled, and Garen buried his brothers-in-arms where they had fallen.

Following the attack, Garen sought out the families of his comrades to deliver news of their deaths, and to return the silver blades bestowed upon them as members of the Dauntless Vanguard. The blades were a token to offer gratitude and protection, but Garen knew it was small comfort after losing a loved one. After delivering three of the daggers, Garen stopped for a drink at the Raptor's Rest, an inn in the hamlet of Brookhollow. Though he tried to keep to himself, feeling the burden of the last dagger he was carrying, he was still recognized as a Crownguard by the innkeeper, who inadvertently announced his presence to the entire room. Garen went outside to leave, but was confronted by the brother of one of his comrades, whom he had delivered a dagger to earlier. The boy publicly blamed Garen for his brother's death and attempted to fight him, angered that Garen had spared one of the Noxians. Garen did not try to fight back, and when the boy threatened Garen with his brother's dagger, Garen offered his own life if it would bring him peace. The boy, unwilling to actually kill Garen, instead broke down into tears, and Garen comforted him with advice on how to move forward. Garen then left Brookhollow on horseback, resolving to find the strength to journey on himself.

After delivering the daggers of the fallen, Garen finally returned home, where his aunt Tianna was instructing Lux in a lesson on Demacian history. Having received word of the ambush, Lux was immediately worried about Garen and was relieved when he said he was unharmed. Garen offered to assist Lux in the lesson, which involved battles and strategy, but Lux still struggled to see herself as one who could follow in the footsteps of Garen and Tianna, to physically serve the kingdom on the battlefield. Garen advised Lux that she should just be herself, and taking his advice, she decided she should volunteer with the Illuminators. Garen believed that charity was no substitute for their duties as Crownguards, but suggested she speak with the Buvelles if she was interested in volunteer work.

children.title: Promotion to Sword-Captain
children.textContent:
As one of the Dauntless Vanguard, Garen proved an exceptional member in their ranks, and served alongside many soldiers he considered his friends. One of these soldiers was Taric, a gentle warrior of immense martial skill, and one Garen knew had the potential to be one of Valoran's greatest heroes, if he devoted himself more wholeheartedly to his kingdom and duties. After Taric was seconded to serve the Vanguard's sword-captain, however, he was found to have abandoned his post. The sword-captain and a dozen other men were slain, and Taric was scheduled to be executed for neglecting his duty.

After the sword-captain's death, Garen was put forward by his fellow warriors to take his place, and the nomination went unopposed. Garen did not wish to see his friend executed though, and as an act of mercy, he instead sentenced him to endure "the Crown of Stone", an old Demacian tradition where the dishonored would be sent to scale Mount Targon, and likely perish on the climb. While the sentence Garen had given would have normally amounted to exile, Taric swore to atone for what he had done and took a ship south to Targon.

children.title: Encounter with the Hag
children.textContent:
When a fellow Demacian soldier had been captured by an old witch in a cave, Garen charged in to rescue him, slaying the sentinels who were guarding the entrance. This witch was known as the Rock Hag, but not wanting to be impolite in her home, Garen referred to her as "the Lady of Stones." As the hag was holding the other soldier hostage, Garen put away his sword and decided to bargain with her, despite believing the encounter would end with one of them dead, and despite not even knowing the other soldier's name.

The hag was known for using magic to sustain herself, by feeding off the memories of others. As such, she declared that she would return Garen's lost soldier if he could offer a single memory she found more delicious. Garen listed several memories the hag could take—him and Lux playing with their uncle, or his grief at the uncle's death—but she was unsatisfied with these options. Garen then asked the hag to search his mind for anything she wished. She sifted through several of his memories, including his victory at Whiterock, a lieutenant's wedding feast, and a comrade dying in his arms at the fields of Brashmore. The hag eventually decided to steal a memory of Lux, when Garen had first discovered and ignored the fact that she was a mage. Garen had many memories just like it, and the hag wanted them all, despite their deal saying she could only take one.

Believing Garen would not be able to recall his missing memories, the hag stole all his memories of Lux and then relinquished her prisoner, satisfied with the meal. As Garen turned to leave though, he realized he felt far too light and unburdened and ran the hag through with his sword, demanding she give them back, as everything now felt wrong. The hag obeyed, but was curious why Garen had even bargained when he was strong enough to kill her at any moment. Garen stated that as part of his personal code, he would always give a stranger a chance before spilling blood in their home. Garen then brought the lost soldier back to Demacia.

children.title: Dragon at Ehrenmount
children.textContent: While at a Demacian training arena in Ehrenmount, Garen and Fiora caught two young children, Aron and Brit, playing with the equipment and pretending to be true heroes of Demacia. These children idolized Garen and Fiora respectively, but had difficulty imitating their moves. Garen jokingly asked Fiora if they should consider them for the Dauntless Vanguard. She declared that seeking glory was futile without first finding excellence, or else they might suffer a violent death. Garen believed this was harsh and started to scold her, but before they could continue the conversation, a large dragon was spotted by the guards and they went to defend the city.

children.title: Haunting of Fossbarrow
children.textContent:
When the magistrate of Fossbarrow's young son Luca had gone missing, Garen and four others from the Dauntless Vanguard—Diadoro, Sabator, Varya, and Rodian—arrived to help search for him. As he had not spent the night in Fossbarrow, Garen and his fellows were unaware of the nightmarish curse which was haunting the city, and he believed Luca had simply run away from home, or this was a routine case of a young Demacian boy getting lost trying to go on a heroic adventure.

While searching the woods for Luca, Garen instead found Lux, who claimed she had come to Fossbarrow to pay her respects to their great-grandfather Fossian, a famed demon slayer. Garen said he was surprised by this, as he recalled their mother saying that Lux hated their last visit to the tomb. As they spoke on the matter while searching for Luca, Lux brought up the possibility that he had been kidnapped by a dark mage. Garen thought this was unlikely, as Varya and Rodian had not seen any evidence of sorcery during their last visit six months prior.

From the distance, Sabator spotted a disturbance in the town, and Garen rode with Lux and his fellow soldiers back to Fossbarrow. A man named Aldo Dayan had gone mad from paranoia and murdered his family and several of his neighbors. Hundreds of the townsfolk had devolved into a terrified mob, believing him a servant of evil and demanding for his execution. Magistrate Giselle, fearing the worst for her son, demanded to know if Dayan killed her son for being "different", but would not elaborate what she meant by this. Disgusted by the murderer, Garen beheaded Dayan after the magistrate ordered his execution, despite Lux's attempts to stop him. Inhuman darkness left the man's body, then the bodies of the other townsfolk, who all fell unconscious. The shadows formed into the 4 dark manifestations of all their worst nightmares and attacked Garen and Lux.

As the shadowhost advanced, Garen deduced that the city was indeed haunted by sorcery. Lux had a plan to put an end to the madness, and as she rode off to Fossian's tomb, Garen placed all his trust in her, commanding the Vanguard to stand strong against the army of horrors. The monsters were relentless, with every nightmare replaced by another the moment it was slain. As the Vanguard were pushed to their limit, and asked how much longer they would have to fight, Garen looked out to the distance, where a brilliant light was shining in the forest. He replied that they would give Lux as long as she needed.

Finally, when it seemed Garen and the other Vanguard were about to die, the shadows all dissipated. Garen was not sure what Lux had done, but somehow her plan had worked. Not only had she had banished the curse from Fossbarrow, but she had also found the young Luca and returned him safely to his mother. A funeral was held later for Aldo Dayan, whom Garen had executed by the magistrate's orders, and the townsfolk all recognized the role they had played in his death. At his burial, Lux asked the Veiled One to look over him. Garen wondered if it was truly just to show mercy on a murderer, but Lux argued that Dayan had been a good friend and neighbor to these people for years, and yet they had rushed to execute him without first understanding why he had turned. Somberly, Garen declared that there was often no room for understanding or nuance. Demacia was a kingdom besieged by enemies from all sides and dark sorcerers from within, and any moment of hesitation would leave them vulnerable. The cost was a sacrifice Garen had to make as part of his oaths for Demacia.

children.title: Escape of the Unshackled
children.textContent:
Early the next day, as Sylas' execution was being carried out in the Grand Plaza, Garen received word from Captain Arrika that Lux had broken out of the Crownguard manor, and after the guards had tried to arrest her, a mysterious woman had intervened to help her escape. An explosion was seen at the site of Sylas' execution, and realizing Lux was there, Garen rushed to the scene, posting two of his men—Vosk and Foulcher—to guard the eastern and western walls.

In the Grand Plaza, Garen found the entire area destroyed, with numerous Demacians dead and crushed by rubble. Sylas was there, along with all the mages he had just broken out from the mageseeker compound. Enraged, Garen demanded to know what Sylas had done to Lux. Sylas replied that he had done nothing; she was the one who came to him. Although Garen physically overpowered Sylas, and his supply of Lux's magic had run out, Sylas was able to defeat Garen by absorbing the magic within his broadsword, Judgment, mocking him for being unaware of his own weapon's power. Judgment's magic left Garen badly injured and unconscious, but Lux was able to convince Sylas to spare Garen's life.

After Lux carried him to safety, Garen recovered in the Infirmary of the Sisters. The doctor was unable to wake him up using traditional medicine, so Tianna, who had come to check on her nephew, decided to approach the situation as a soldier instead of a healer. As he lay asleep, Tianna whispered a stern order to him: his King was in danger, and his nation was under attack. Garen had sworn to protect them, and after hearing this, he stirred awake.

As Sylas and his insurrection continued to cause chaos in the Great City, Garen received a briefing from Captain Jaredan, who claimed that one of Sylas' mages was fleeing the capital along the rooftops, after helping to free him from his execution. Despite his injury, Garen caught the mysterious woman as she made her escape, unaware that she had just assassinated King Jarvan III. Garen and Katarina crossed blades briefly as he tried to apprehend her, but Katarina explained that Jaredan had lied to him: she was not a mage, and was actually the one who rescued Lux. Garen still believed he had a duty to take her in, but after seeing another explosion by the royal palace, he realized he was wasting time while Lux was in danger. He asked who Katarina really was before she left, but she would not tell him, claiming they both knew she would only lie to him.

children.title: Death of the King
children.textContent:
Upon reaching the Citadel of Dawn, Garen found Lux and Jarvan IV being held by Sylas and the other mages. After fighting his way through, Garen did battle with Sylas once more. Lux warned him not to use his sword, and though Garen was confused by this, he sheathed Judgment, instead borrowing a halberd from one of his guards. After Lux freed the prince, Sylas was forced to flee the scene. Jarvan wanted to pursue Sylas for murdering his father, but Garen told him to get to safety, as the kingdom needed him alive.

Having learned about the King's death, Garen pursued Sylas, believing he was the one who killed him. Sylas claimed that while he had planned to execute the king, he was already dead when he found him, which meant it must have been one of the other mages rampaging through the Great City. Garen did not believe him, and that even if this were true, Sylas still needed to pay for all the destruction he had caused, and for betraying Lux. But Sylas mocked Garen's sense of honor; he had known all along that Lux was a mage, yet he continued to lock up so many people like her. For this, Lux could not even trust her own brother and had been hiding her magic from him for years. Garen flew into a rage and continued his battle with Sylas, this time defeating him with Judgment's power. When he was about to land the killing blow, however, Sylas escaped with the help of a mageseeker named Killan, and finally fled the capital. Garen and the other soldiers lost track of his whereabouts, but the search for the "kingslayer" continued.

The funeral for King Jarvan III was held the day after Sylas escaped. During his eulogy, Jarvan IV declared that as the new king, he would not rest until his father's killer was brought to justice, and he formally declared war on "the scourge of magic" in Demacia. The announcement preceded a mass expansion of the mageseekers and their arrests, with even loyal Demacians being ripped from their homes and tossed onto the streets. Garen and Lux were both disturbed by Jarvan's decree, but while Lux wanted to help the other mages, Garen was even more terrified and insisted that she needed to focus on protecting herself. The best way to do that, Garen claimed, was for the two of them to help Jarvan and the mageseekers make the arrests, which would allow them to keep all fingers pointed away from her and toward someone else. Lux was disgusted by his suggestion, claiming that the Garen she knew would rather die than go along with this plan.

Garen, under direct orders from Jarvan, continued to help the mageseekers make the arrests. One of these was a boy who knew where the other mages were hiding, and after interrogating him, Garen followed a trail alone into the sewers; the route Sylas had escaped through before he destroyed the exit. At the end of the tunnel, and to his dismay, he found Lux leading a group of mage refugees out of the city. Garen was upset that Lux had not followed his advice, believing he had given her a way out if she had only decided to help the mageseekers. His orders from Jarvan were to arrest the mages and bring them back to the city, but Lux declared that if he was going to do this, he would have to arrest her too. Using her staff, she blasted a massive hole in the rubble, opening the exit for the other mages to go through. Garen, stunned, could only stand and watch as they left, and for the first time, Lux got him to verbally acknowledge to her what he had always known deep down: she was a mage, and also a Demacian. After a tearful hug and goodbye, Lux left the Great City of Demacia with the other refugees.

After Lux and her mages had escaped from the Great City, Garen lied to Jarvan in order to protect her, and she was declared 'missing' in the following months. Sylas' insurrection in the capital expanded into a nationwide conflict, with the rebel mages employing guerilla tactics to cut away at their numbers. Jarvan was no longer himself, and Garen put the blame on himself for not being there to protect the king. The hunt for Sylas continued to no avail, and Garen lost more and more soldiers who had been placed under his command, numbering to thirty-seven total.

children.title: Expedition to the Nockmirch
children.textContent:
Some months after the death of Jarvan III, and almost four years after Garen's promotion to sword-captain, High Marshal Tianna Crownguard and the Silver Council received a letter from the Demacian ambassador to the Nockmirch, Garen's great uncle Hargold. Tianna believed the letter to be a forgery, so she summoned Garen to the council room, where she gave him a mission to check and see if Hargold was alive and well. Garen, who had been distracted and exhausted since the king's assassination, believed he was still needed in Demacia to search for Sylas. But to Tianna, the Vanguard's recent casualties on Garen's watch proved that he needed some time away from the conflict so he could return to Demacia at full strength. Garen still disagreed with the orders, but decided he should not disobey his superior officer.

Garen took three of the Dauntless Vanguard's Shields with him: the First, the Fourth, and the Eighth. Each company brought with it eight soldiers, with the First Shield consisting of Shield-Sergeant Merrek, the Stonehorn minotaur Kriel, master swordswoman Alys Morn, the affable Aiguo, the surly Eben Hess, the twins Saskja and Rurik Vos, and Shield's newest member, young Cithria of Cloudfield, who had replaced veteran soldier Helmar after she passed her Testing with scores comparable to Garen's own. They took a ship called the Stormbright south along the Ironfork River, into the Bay of Sentinels, and finally eastward along the Silverrun, taking shifts rowing until they reached the Demacian border at the Custodian Wall. Along the way, they picked up Quinn close to Everpeak. The ranger-knight had also been sent by Tianna to join them on the diplomatic mission, and Garen was happy to have her join them.

Using Quinn's knowledge of the border nations, the Vanguard made their way to Alderburg, which was the seat of power of the Nockmirch and their leader, the Lord Castellan Eldwyn. When they reached the ravine between the Nockmirch and the Rijenland, however, Garen was surprised to see an enormous bridge had been built, suitable for an army to be marched across with very little warning. The Silver Council had not been informed of this bridge by their ambassador, and to Garen's knowledge, it appeared to have been designed for a westward invasion into the Rijenland, their own ally. Concerned, Garen took Quinn and the Vanguard across the bridge to find his great-uncle Hargold. They were stopped by the guards, who then brought out the city's warden, a warrior named Vigrid.

As they were both soldiers, Garen got along fairly well with Vigrid, who offered a feast of welcome for the Demacians. However, Garen was wary about what happened to his uncle and allowed Quinn to sneak outside the city to investigate. During the feast, Lord Eldwyn was very dismissive and rude to the Demacians, despite having been a long-time friend of Jarvan III. The lord's wife had died somewhat recently, and his sour mood left the atmosphere tense. The lord's daughter, Lady Odelyn, tried to extend hospitality toward Garen and condolences for the loss of their king, but got in an argument with her father and Vigrid and was quickly sent away to her room. The seat for the ambassador Hargold remained empty during the banquet, and having had enough of Eldwyn's lack of manners, Garen demanded he be allowed to speak with him.

To Garen's surprise, the Nockmirchers brought out Hargold, who was apparently safe and sound. Garen remembered Hargold from when he was young, but his behavior was off. When Garen began to recite the Crownguard family motto, Hargold ignored him instead of completing it, and when Garen asked why he had not informed them of the massive bridge Alderburg had constructed, or the death of Lord Eldwyn's wife, Hargold blamed his recent illness before changing the topic to talk about the weather. Just then, Quinn arrived into the hall. The Nockmirchers tried to prevent her from reaching Garen, but failed, and she told him that Noxians were camped just outside Alderburg. Without another word, Garen declared they were leaving at once. When Hargold refused to go with them, Garen grew suspicious and asked him to recite the Crownguard family motto. He was unable to, and Garen declared that this was not Hargold, but instead a deceiver; a mage disguised as the Demacian ambassador. The sorceress escaped and quickly vanished from view, and Garen realized they had walked right into a trap: Warden Vigrid was secretly a Noxian, and Lord Eldwyn had betrayed his alliance with Demacia to align himself with the empire.

As a massive Noxian warhost marched towards Alderburg, Garen and the Vanguard tried to flee the city and get word back to Demacia. Only Valor managed to escape, with Quinn being gravely wounded on the watchtower, and the Vanguard being trapped on the bridge across the ravine. What followed was an onslaught by the Noxians, as Warden Vigrid sent wave after wave of his warriors, each one more powerful than the last. One wave included a chieftain riding a basilisk, who had nearly killed Garen before Quinn intervened with a well-timed bolt from her crossbow. Garen lost several soldiers in the battle, including Aiguo, Rurik Vos, and Shield-Sergeant Rosk of Eighth Shield. After killing the chieftain, and realizing the next wave would consist of the most elite Noxian warriors, Garen decided it was time for the Vanguard to launch an offensive. During this battle, Garen dueled and managed to kill Vigrid, though his armor was badly dented and his arm was broken. The Noxians kept fighting after their leader was defeated, but were forced to flee when Valor arrived with the Demacian 4 raptor-knights as reinforcements.

After the battle was over, Lord Eldwyn was captured and brought to Garen, who was to decide his fate. The Lord Castellan thought he had no choice but to side with Noxus; Demacia never responded to their pleas for aid when they were under attack, which were meant to be sent through the ambassador. The mage disguised as Hargold had escaped, and many people had died as a result of the Lord Castellan's actions. And although Noxus had been responsible for the death of the ambassador and breakdown in communication, Eldwyn had still broken his oaths and alliance with Demacia. After asking Cithria's opinion on the matter, Garen decided that they should spare Eldwyn's life, on the condition that he abdicate as lord and relinquish his titles to his daughter Odelyn, not wanting to destroy centuries-old alliance on account of the actions of one man. Garen also promised that Demacia would be a more faithful ally to the Nockmirch and would help strengthen their defenses, and left Kriel in the Nockmirch as the interim ambassador until a replacement for Hargold had been chosen. As the Vanguard departed Alderburg, Garen saw a red-eyed raven in the distance watching them, and he vowed that Demacia would be ready for any war with Noxus.

Upon returning to the Great City, Garen spoke with Tianna again regarding the outcome of his mission. Quinn was still recovering from her wounds, but had restored enough of her strength to leave the city despite the healers trying to stop her. Hargold was dead, as were a number of the Vanguard. Tianna commended Garen, as he had uncovered a major Noxian conspiracy, and she believed it was a miracle any of them had survived. She was also impressed with his decision to install Lady Odelyn as leader of the Nockmirch; Tianna would have simply imprisoned her and Eldwyn to install a puppet ruler, but Garen's approach had made the Nockmirch into a much stronger ally. Still, Garen worried that the Nockmirchers might one day turn on Odelyn for standing against Noxus, which would make them the front line for a Demacian-Noxian war. As a final note, Tianna informed Garen that the Silver Council was unnerved that one of their ambassadors had been replaced with a mage. The revelation had only strengthened the position of the mageseekers in the royal court, and Tianna feared that Jarvan's anger made him easy to manipulate so long as Sylas remained at large. Tianna then gave Garen a new dispatch with their latest intelligence, and Garen set out to find Sylas once more.

children.title: The Mageseeker
children.textContent:
During the Great Mage Rebellion, Sylas continued to deal numerous blows against Demacia and its Mageseekers, recruiting prisoners and growing the rebellion in the settlements of Thornbarrow, Clarion Cove, Devineur, and Glyndemere. After the fall of Capelworth, Garen met with Jarvan IV in the palace war room to discuss the growing chaos. The young king was frustrated by his own inability to bring peace to his realm, and believed his father would be ashamed to see Sylas running free and killing Demacians. Despite this, Garen believed that Jarvan would prevail; his father had overcome countless struggles in his time, and all they needed for victory was to defeat one man. Jarvan thanked Garen for his confidence, and the sword-captain left to continue his search for the rebels.

Some time later, Garen encountered Sylas once more in the Great City, which was on high alert after Sylas raided Hesbeth's laboratory in the Mageseeker headquarters. Sylas defeated Garen once again and prepared to finish him, but was stopped by the rebel leader Leilani, who argued that Lux would never help them if Sylas killed her brother. Begrudgingly, Sylas spared Garen, who promptly collapsed to the ground from exhaustion. Later, when Garen regained consciousness, he got word that his uncle Eldred, the Mageseeker leader, had been killed, and Sylas had escaped once again.

Following the attack, Jarvan IV met with Garen and Commander Wisteria, the new Mageseeker leader after Eldred's death. Angry that Sylas escaped once again, Jarvan declared that he had thus far been too lenient and ordered Wisteria to arrest every last mage in the kingdom. Garen believed this was going too far, but Wisteria argued that imprisonment was not going far enough, instead suggesting that the mages should be executed in case they be freed and recruited by the rebellion. Jarvan believed this was extreme, but believed he had no choice now that war had gripped every corner of the kingdom and approved the request, informing Garen that if Lux returned home to the capital, then he would use his authority as king to protect her.

Some time later, Garen spoke with Wisteria in the palace war room. The mageseeker leader inquired if he had any recent contact with Lux, and he replied that he had not. She then claimed to have received intelligence that Lux was harboring terrorist mages in Terbisia on behalf of Sylas, and to Garen's horror, the mageseekers would use their newfound authority to lead an invasion of the ruins and make an example of every mage there. Fearing for his sister's safety, Garen traveled at once to Terbisia and met with Lux to warn her about the attack. He tried to convince Lux to return home and evacuate the refugees, but Lux informed him the mages would all be killed if they returned to Demacia—they would make their stand in Terbisia, and Lux refused to abandon them. Garen acknowledged that he had let her down, and after a brief argument, he obeyed when she told him to go back to his king.

Garen met with Jarvan IV to inform him that Lux had refused to come back with him. In turn, Jarvan informed him that Shyvana had left Demacia for fear of the new laws. The two friends lamented on their failure to protect their loved ones, and though Garen tried to assure Jarvan that he had been trying to be a good king, Jarvan disagreed. Jarvan confessed that before his father died, he had written a decree which would have ended the mageseekers' operations in Demacia. After the king's death, however, Jarvan had secretly found and burned it, unwilling to forgive what Sylas had done. Garen then reflected on when he had first discovered Lux was a mage, and realized that though he had been afraid for her, he had been more afraid of his own social standing. He advised Jarvan that if the people they loved had magic, then they had to be protected. Jarvan agreed and ordered Garen to Terbisia's defense, where he was to command Wisteria to stand down and deliver a message to Shyvana should he find her.

During the battle, Lux had been nearly overwhelmed defending Terbisia from the invasion when she was rescued by Garen, who arrived to fight by her side. Garen apologized for not being on her side from the beginning and said that he and Jarvan would end the arrests, now seeing the mageseekers as the real threat to Demacia. Lux forgave him, and after fending off the remaining monsters, the invasion finally ended in Wisteria's death at the hands of Sylas. Garen remained in the city to help them rebuild, and the siblings began to reconnect after months apart. Garen knew Sylas would not end his rebellion and believed he should be stopped, but Lux was more willing to look past his faults for his role in defeating the mageseekers. Garen also believed Jarvan still needed some sense talked into him and requested Lux return to the capital, as unlike her, he lacked the people skills to function as a proper advisor. Lux said that she would on the condition that Garen not try to betrothe her to Jarvan again. Later, Garen was seen with Lux and Jarvan looking out on the horizon to see Shyvana returning back to Demacia.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent: Garen is a tall, stocky, and muscular man with fair skin, a strong jaw, short brown hair, and blue eyes. As Sword-Captain of the Dauntless Vanguard, he is usually seen wearing the armor standard for his station: Demacian silver and gold which covers his forearms and torso, and large brown boots and gloves. Around his neck, Garen wears a Crownguard crest clasping the blue cloak which hangs down his back. On his shoulders, Garen wears the massive battle pauldrons which the Crownguards are known for, which are each adorned with a large blue gemstone in the front. His massive broadsword, Judgment, bears the same color pattern and gemstone as his pauldrons, and due to its size it is either held at his side or sheathed behind his back.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent:
As one of the Crownguard family, Garen was raised to be a disciplined and honorable protector of his king and country, though he was known to have been somewhat brash in his youth. As a soldier, Garen approached each situation with a stoic determination, which he used to inspire others and to mask the grief of each comrade lost. Garen was often uncomfortable with lying or keeping secrets, or being impolite to people he had just met. His sense of justice was clear and to the point; those who harmed innocents or acted dishonorably would have to answer to his blade. Garen devoted himself fully to Demacia and was deeply protective of the people he loved, including his friends Jarvan IV and Quinn, and especially his younger sister Luxanna. Unlike Lux, Garen rarely questioned the kingdom's laws or authority. His grief at losing his uncle to a rogue mage, and the fear he saw in Lux's eyes, led Garen to distrust magic. But when he learned that Lux herself was a mage, Garen feared confronting reality for a long time. The glaring contradiction in his beliefs eventually forced Garen to reexamine his own values, and those of the kingdom he had sworn to protect.

As the leader of the Dauntless Vanguard, Garen felt a great sense of responsibility for the safety of Demacia and its people. After the King's assassination and the ensuing civil war, his aunt Tianna criticized him for always trying to shoulder far more than he was able to carry on his own. Whenever he was stressed or confused, Garen would throw himself into his duties and training. He was also known to stand with and defend his fellow soldiers, and made an effort to get to know the people placed under his command. He had a good memory and tried to learn each of their names whenever he could, and was able to recall the exact number of his soldiers that were killed in action. He was also hard on the Vanguard while training them to ensure they would survive on the battlefield. Most believed Garen to be an excellent leader, but he also had not sought out this role, sometimes wishing for the days when he was just a soldier following orders.

children.title: Lux Crownguard
children.textContent: Garen is Lux's older brother and their family name is Crownguard. He deeply respects and loves his sister, but he fears what may happen to her if her magical abilities were to be revealed. He and their aunt, Tianna Crownguard, planned on arranging Lux to marry prince Jarvan IV in order to protect her from persecution. After the events of the Lux comic, Lux got Garen to verbally acknowledge that she was a mage. The two parted ways on good terms. In the end, Garen ended up accepting his magician sister and that not all magicians are bad people. Garen then found and reunited with Lux in Terbisia. However, the two have a fall out due to the events of the Great Mage Rebellion. Garen was then able to reconcile with his sister, vowing to never abandon her again as they stand together against the Mageseekers. As their family name implies, they serve and protect the crown.

children.title: Katarina Du Couteau
children.textContent: Garen and Katarina first met one another at the beginning of Sylas' mage rebellion, as the injured Garen was rushing into battle from the infirmary and she was escaping after assassinating Jarvan III. The two crossed blades briefly, with Garen unaware that she was a Noxian and believing she was one of Sylas' mages, as Captain Jaredan had informed him. Garen was offended when Katarina described Sylas as " Lux's favorite criminal", and was worried Katarina had hurt her, but Katarina said she was actually the one who rescued Lux, as Captain Arrika had reported to him earlier. Garen still believed he needed to arrest Katarina, but let her go when he saw another magical blast by the royal palace and rushed to the scene. When he asked for Katarina's name, she refused to tell him, as they both knew she would only lie to him.

children.title: Jarvan Lightshield IV
children.textContent:
Garen met Jarvan IV after he joined the military as a squire, and as brothers-in-arms, they became close friends. Garen looked up to Jarvan's abilities as a tactician, and Jarvan looked up to Garen's raw determination as a soldier. The two made an excellent team, and Garen always knew growing up that as one of the Crownguard family, it would someday be his duty to defend the prince once he was crowned king. When Garen and Jarvan were older, they frequently went sparring together in the royal palace. Although they were friends, Garen would speak formally with the prince in accordance with protocol, calling him "my liege" even though Jarvan preferred to be called by name. Garen also knew that Jarvan loved Shyvana, but believed the prince needed to move on from not being allowed to marry her.

After Jarvan became king, Garen believed Jarvan was no longer himself as he grieved his father's assassination, and was disturbed by how the mageseekers were able to manipulate him. Garen disobeyed some of Jarvan's orders to protect Lux, but also felt a great deal of shame for lying to a man who was both his sworn lord and best friend.

children.title: Taric
children.textContent: Garen and Taric are long-time friends from the Shield of Valoran's days in the Demacian military. Garen was furious at being convinced Taric was wasting his talents as a warrior but he still believed in him.

children.title: Pieter Crownguard
children.textContent: Pieter Crownguard is a decorated officer in the Demacian military, and is the father of Garen and Lux. Garen believes his father is a kind and forgiving man, standing in sharp contrast to his sister Tianna, Garen's aunt.

children.title: Nocturne
children.textContent: Nocturne haunted and terrorized the Demacian city of Fossbarrow until Lux banished the demon from the neighboring tomb of her great-grandfather Fossian, with the help of Garen and the Dauntless Vanguard. Although Garen fought the army of nightmarish shadows summoned by Nocturne, he assumed they were the work of a dark sorcerer, as did most Demacians who heard about the incident after it had occurred.

children.title: Sylas
children.textContent: Garen has fought Sylas and his allies multiple times.

children.title: Galio
children.textContent: Garen encountered Galio when his sister summoned the Colossus to fight off Sylas and the Winter's Claw.

children.title: Fiora Laurent
children.textContent: Garen and Fiora know each other, as she helps train the Dauntless Vanguard, though they never train together as "the outcome would be obvious". They fought together against the dragon that attacked Ehrenmount.

mainContent.title: Change log
mainContent.textContent:
The following change log is for the Character blurb found at the top of the article:

For historical versions of other stories, please see each story's respective change log.
